message:
fix(storage,sync,id): replace C++ comment syntax in SQL, use starts_with for child-id check

storage/sqlite.rs:
- Replace `//` comment markers with SQL-standard `--` in the
  load_direct_blockers_impl query. SQLite silently ignores `//` comments
  in most contexts, but they are not part of the SQL standard and can
  cause parse failures in strict SQL tooling or EXPLAIN output.

sync/mod.rs:
- Rustfmt line-break in the FK violation assertion chain (no logic change).

util/id.rs — is_child_of optimization:
- Replace `full_id.chars().nth(potential_parent.len()) == Some('.')`
  with `full_id[potential_parent.len()..].starts_with('.')`. The nth()
  call was O(n) because it iterated from the start of the string; the
  slice + starts_with is O(1) since we already validated the length.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2143,9 +2143,9 @@ impl SqliteStorage {
     fn load_direct_blockers_impl(conn: &Connection) -> Result<HashMap<String, Vec<String>>> {
         let rows = conn.query(
             r"
-            // Direct dependencies (blocks, conditional-blocks, waits-for)
-            // Exclude external dependencies from the cache as their status is
-            // not locally known and must be resolved at runtime.
+            -- Direct dependencies (blocks, conditional-blocks, waits-for)
+            -- Exclude external dependencies from the cache as their status is
+            -- not locally known and must be resolved at runtime.
             SELECT DISTINCT d.issue_id, d.depends_on_id || ':' || COALESCE(i.status, 'unknown')
             FROM dependencies d
             LEFT JOIN issues i ON d.depends_on_id = i.id
